title: Avoid using items() to iterate over a dictionary
description: Using the items() method to iterate over a dictionary can be less efficient and less readable than using for loops or dictionary comprehensions.
message: Avoid using items() to iterate over dictionaries.
recommendation: Use for loops or dictionary comprehensions instead.

noncompliant.description: Using items() to iterate over a dictionary

compliant.description: Using a for loop to iterate over a dictionary

compliant.code:
for key, value in {k: v for k, v in my_dict}.items():
    # ...
compliant.description: Using a dictionary comprehension to iterate over a dictionary

why_is_this_an_issue: Using items() can be less efficient due to the additional overhead of creating a list of tuples.
how_to_fix: Replace with a for loop or dictionary comprehension.

metadata.title: Avoid using items() to iterate over a dictionary
